title: Openapi Docs Route
description: SpecD Studio capability **Openapi Docs Route** (`api:openapi-docs-route`). Expose generated OpenAPI and optional Swagger UI for integrators. This spec is canonical after archive: implementers rely on the requirements below, not change-only documents.
context: SpecD Studio capability **Openapi Docs Route** (`api:openapi-docs-route`). Expose generated OpenAPI and optional Swagger UI for integrators. This spec is canonical after archive: implementers rely on the requirements below, not change-only documents.

rules.requirement: openapi.json is served at a documented path
rules:
The API MUST expose an HTTP `GET` endpoint that returns the generated **OpenAPI 3.1** document as JSON.

The path MUST be documented in repository docs (for example `docs/api/openapi.md`) and MAY change between releases.
The server MAY expose an interactive documentation UI (e.g. Swagger UI), but it is not required for Studio v1.

constraints: HTTP handlers MUST NOT import @specd/core from @specd/ui or @specd/client.
v1 server auth: api.auth.type from specd.yaml (never studio.*); registry registers only disabled; no server-side Bearer enforcement on loopback or specd ui serve.
Artifact save/load MUST use core:save-change-artifact and core:get-change-artifact — not raw ChangeRepository.saveArtifact from HTTP handlers.
There is no GET /changes/{name}/validation resource; use GET .../status and POST .../validate.
Canonical workspace spec artifacts are read-only in Studio v1.

scenarios.requirement: openapi.json is served at a documented path
scenarios.name: OpenAPI document available at documented endpoint
when: client fetches the documented OpenAPI endpoint

scenarios.requirement: openapi.json is served at a documented path
scenarios.name: Document describes the v1 contract surface
then: the document describes the Studio API contract (routes, DTO schemas, auth schemes)

scenarios.requirement: openapi.json is served at a documented path
scenarios.name: Document endpoint survives server restart
then: AND schema version field is present

scenarios.name: No interactive docs UI is required
then: the OpenAPI JSON endpoint remains available regardless of whether any interactive docs UI is mounted
